operating_state: Third quarter 2024 earnings reached $4.4 billion as reported in August, reflecting continued operational performance across the integrated energy portfolio. Asset retirement obligations decreased to $12.4 billion from $13.8 billion at year-start, driven by $1.8 billion in settlements during the nine-month period, partially offset by $441 million in accretion expense.
cost_structure: Asset retirement obligation liabilities were settled at $1.8 billion during the nine months, representing increased decommissioning activity compared to $1.1 billion in the prior year period. Accretion expense on retirement obligations totaled $441 million, down from $457 million in the comparable period, reflecting the impact of settlements on the liability base.
material_events: The Federal Trade Commission completed its review of the Hess acquisition on September 30, 2024, accepting a consent agreement that prohibits John B. Hess from joining Chevron's board following merger completion. The HSR Act waiting period expired July 1, 2024, providing one year to close the transaction subject to Stabroek Block arbitration resolution.
macro_exposures: The Hess merger faces continued uncertainty from ongoing arbitration with Exxon and CNOOC regarding right of first refusal provisions in the Stabroek Block operating agreement offshore Guyana. The arbitration merits hearing is scheduled for May 2025 with a decision expected within three months thereafter.
forward_outlook: Chevron and Hess remain committed to completing the merger following satisfactory resolution of the Stabroek Block arbitration proceedings. The companies will take appropriate steps to maintain HSR clearance for merger closing, including filing additional HSR notifications if necessary before the one-year deadline.
uncertainty_profile: Merger completion remains contingent on resolving the Stabroek Block joint operating agreement arbitration, with Chevron and Hess maintaining confidence that the right of first refusal does not apply to the transaction. The arbitration timeline extends the merger uncertainty through mid-2025, with potential for additional regulatory filings to preserve antitrust clearance.
